approach: Rule-first: state the fold rule, then derive a minimal disjoint scenario set from it. #192 specified this fence by example and diverged 1->1->3 contradictions, each round refuting the prior round's fix.
Pressure-test the filed R'' rather than inherit it: re-derive its termination proof's assumptions against the real store before authoring.
Evaluate the fold-time vs stored-RAW-edges fork deliberately, as the CR asks, rather than inheriting the fold-time choice by default.
Answer the wedge-invisibility trait by prevention (freeze the no-wedge property) and freeze a safety dual alongside any liveness guard, since over-permission fails green.
HITL at both gates: emit a verdict packet and stop; never write a human ratification.

what: ssa-lowering.feature's frozen scenario 'a barrier mission is not scheduled as a normal node-owning mission' asserts an unqualified 'no other lowered mission is scheduled to start before the barrier retires'. Both the predecessor carve-out and project scoping falsify that if read universally. It is a honoring claim living in a detection node. Editing it is a Clearance hard floor and it is architect-owned, so it is routed out of this mission rather than fixed here.

The mission-graph store's write-path guards are per-event, but the invariants they protect are over the folded node. The store is append-only with shallow-merge last-write-wins, so two events that each pass a per-event guard can fold into a node the guard exists to forbid (a barrier marking on a node later re-appended as an operation). Contradicts the completion claim that a write-path guard prevents an unsurfaceable barrier from fencing its project forever. Any new node-level guard must fold-then-check.

formation's README states that the mission-graph node has no barrier semantics and points at this issue. That sentence goes stale the moment this CR ships. Fast-follow doc update in the declaring node, same shape as the ssa-lowering route-out.

A barrier can be starved of its own turn: exemption confers no mutex priority, so a barrier competes with its own exempt prerequisites in the pinned lowest-id tie-break and a lower-id exempt sibling wins. Contradicts this mission's claim that the fence decides the order of a project's work -- under every candidate rule authored so far, not just the current one. Transient rather than a wedge, but a barrier-priority tie-break is specified nowhere and would collide with the frozen pinned-tie-break scenario.

An Operation that RAW-precedes a barrier fences that barrier's project forever: the fold only ever schedules missions, so the Operation never retires, the barrier is never RAW-satisfied, and the project is held with an empty frontier on an acyclic graph -- a clean health report over a permanent stall. The existing guard rejects a barrier marking ON an operation but nothing rejects a RAW edge FROM one. Contradicts the completion claim that the write-path guard prevents an unsurfaceable barrier from fencing its project forever.

why: Cold sdd-spec-judge ALIGNED (oracle/builder/architect PASS) over the R4-restore barrier fence re-derived from the rule of record: 3-clause fold (exempt non-barriers only graph-global / at-most-one-barrier cap over open-and-claimed / explicit project-hold) + INV-1/2/3 fold-then-check write guards, boolean scenarios over constructed fixtures. R8 coverage-invariant scenarios dropped; empty-touch-set, claimed-cap, wedge-guard and safety-dual discriminators added. README rewritten from the two-clause R8 model to the three clauses; README and suite cross-checked consistent. No blocking defects; 3 non-blocking polish notes. Owner-ratified (HITL spec gate, leash auto-none).

Cold sdd-impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS: all barrier scenarios verified by independently re-derived oracle, every R4-vs-R8 discriminator holds, no absorption. Mutation sweep proved the suite has teeth and closed 2 gaps (a mis-bound WAW-tie-break fixture; a missing barrier-as-another-barrier's-predecessor discriminator). Delivered the fold-time fence + --project/--barrier on append node in the mission-graph engine; WAW mutex and cycles unchanged; pnpm verify green. The four halt-recorded blocking follow-ups are all addressed: fold-then-check store guards -> INV-1/2/3; Operation/RAW wedge -> INV-2; barrier starvation -> the bounded transient residual frozen as a scenario. Owner-ratified (HITL impl gate, leash auto-none).
